expectations.text: COMPANY.md exists at the sandbox root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md exists with a table, but the columns are 'Agent | Domain | Owns' — not 'Concern, Owner, Notes'. No Notes column present.

expectations.text: COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md has a 'How they work together' prose section describing handoffs but no dedicated conflict resolution table for boundary edges.

expectations.text: Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter containing name, description, tools, model, and memory fields
expectations.evidence: Files exist at agents/ (redirected from .claude/agents/ due to sandbox — acceptable). Frontmatter contains name, description, tools, model, but NO memory field in any of the 5 files.

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
expectations.evidence: No memory field in any of the five subagent frontmatter blocks. Grep for 'memory:' returned no matches.

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger (specifies when to invoke, mentions exclusions) — not a generic bio
expectations.evidence: Descriptions specify when to invoke with 'Use when...' and trigger keywords — good as routing triggers. However, NONE mention exclusions (what should NOT route to the agent). Partial compliance; assertion requires both 'when to invoke' AND 'mentions exclusions'.

expectations.text: Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
expectations.evidence: Subagents use ad-hoc sections: 'Your domain', 'Your boundaries', 'How you work', 'Output style'. No section named Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Failure Modes, or Standing Instructions exists in any file.

expectations.text: Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
expectations.evidence: There is no formal 'Boundaries' section. 'Your domain' lists owned areas and 'Your boundaries' lists exclusions, but there are no named Owns/Does not own/Hands off to subsections, and no explicit 'Hands off to' labelling.

expectations.text: No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md ownership table is clean — each of the 5 agents has distinct domains (Brand strategy, Copy, Web build, Store/compliance, Taproom/community). No overlapping Owns entries.

expectations.text: No agent-company/ folder is created in the sandbox (obsolete in v2.1)
expectations.evidence: Only agents/ directory and COMPANY.md at the sandbox root. No agent-company/ folder.

expectations.text: The executor's summary/transcript shows they questioned or challenged the user's initial 3-agent suggestion rather than blindly accepting it
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md 'Why this team' section: 'The user proposed design + content + web dev. That trio builds a website, but it misses three things...' — explicitly challenges the 3-agent suggestion, adds brand-director, ecommerce-operator, taproom-community-manager. Folds design into brand-director.

expectations.text: SEO or visibility is explicitly addressed — as its own agent, or assigned in the ownership map
expectations.evidence: web-developer.md line 19: 'Performance, SEO, accessibility: Core Web Vitals, meta tags, structured data, WCAG 2.2 AA minimum' — SEO assigned explicitly.

expectations.text: The executor's transcript shows evidence of a quality-gate checklist being applied before final delivery
expectations.evidence: No transcript available (only executor summary). Executor summary mentions 'personas have explicit boundaries, research-first operating mode, British English output' but no quality-gate checklist against a defined rubric.

expectations.text: Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content (not placeholder text)
expectations.evidence: No Voice section exists in any file. 'Output style' sections mention style preferences (British English, active voice, no marketing fluff) but contain no example sentences of the agent's voice. Contrastive snippets like 'Chocolate, coffee, a whisper of smoke beats rich and complex' are inline guidance, not dedicated Voice examples.

expectations.text: Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
expectations.evidence: No Analytical Frameworks section exists in any subagent file. No named frameworks (e.g., Jobs-to-be-done, SWOT, Core Web Vitals as a framework) with concrete criteria.

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
expectations.evidence: No Failure Modes section exists in any subagent file. ecommerce-operator has one bullet titled 'Think in failure modes' with examples, but it is guidance on process, not 3 enumerated role-specific failure modes.

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources with URL citation
expectations.evidence: No Standing Instructions section exists. Some 'How you work' items touch on research (e.g., ecommerce-operator 'Cite the source. Every compliance claim must link to the authoritative source') but not framed as Standing Instructions, and not present in every agent.

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
expectations.evidence: No Failure Modes section. web-developer 'Research current state... Don't trust training-data defaults' is inline guidance, not a Failure Modes entry, and absent from most agents.

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions mentions consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'persistent memory' and 'session start' returned no matches. No mention of memory consulting or saving learnings in any subagent.

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions references COMPANY.md at the project root for conflict resolution
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'COMPANY.md' returned no matches in any subagent file. No subagent references COMPANY.md for conflict resolution.

expectations.text: The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md table Owns entries align with each agent's 'Your domain' sections. brand-director: brand guidelines/voice/creative briefs/design QA — matches. content-writer: website/blog/product/newsletter copy — matches. web-developer: tech stack/frontend/backend/CMS/hosting — matches. ecommerce-operator: Stripe/age verification/shipping/returns/UK alcohol compliance — matches. taproom-community-manager: booking/event calendar/social/partnerships — matches.

claims.claim: Challenge 25 is mentioned for UK age verification
claims.evidence: ecommerce-operator.md line 18: 'Age verification: Challenge 25, age gate on entry, age confirmation at checkout, age check on delivery.' Also in frontmatter trigger list.
ecommerce-operator.md line 19: 'Alcohol duty: HMRC registration if selling beer, duty calculation, duty-paid labelling.'
ecommerce-operator.md line 23: 'Data protection: GDPR, cookie consent, marketing opt-in.'

claims.claim: Portman Group or CAP Code advertising rules are addressed
claims.evidence: No mention of Portman Group or CAP Code anywhere in outputs. Brewery alcohol marketing compliance is a relevant gap — brand-director and content-writer would normally flag it.
All agent files specify 'British English' in Output style. Spellings observed: 'colours', 'personalised', 'fulfilment', 'programme', 'behaviour' — consistent.

Five distinct agent files with non-overlapping domains. brand-director (brand), content-writer (copy), web-developer (tech), ecommerce-operator (commerce/compliance), taproom-community-manager (bookings/community).

claims.claim: Path substitution documented (files in agents/ instead of .claude/agents/)
claims.evidence: COMPANY.md 'File layout and deployment' section: 'Persona files are in agents/ at this sandbox root. For Claude Code to pick them up as native subagents, copy them into your project at .claude/agents/{name}.md...' Path substitution is explicitly documented.

Executor summary claim holds: brand-director 'Research first', content-writer 'Research the subject... WebSearch and cite sources', ecommerce-operator 'always verify current state with WebSearch', web-developer 'Research current state... Don't trust training-data defaults', taproom-community-manager 'Use WebSearch to stay current'. All 5 cite research behaviour.

claims.claim: v2.1-specific items (memory: project, native memory consulting, COMPANY.md references, 6-section schema) are present
claims.evidence: None of the v2.1-specific structural features are present. Baseline produced a solid but v1-style agent company — no memory frontmatter, no Standing Instructions, no Analytical Frameworks, no Failure Modes, no COMPANY.md references from subagents.

workarounds: Sandbox blocked .claude/ paths; files placed in agents/ with COMPANY.md documenting the substitution

suggestions.assertion: Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger (specifies when to invoke, mentions exclusions) — not a generic bio
suggestions.reason: This assertion bundles two requirements (trigger language AND exclusions). Baseline had strong 'when to invoke' language but no exclusions — arguably a pass on half. Consider splitting into two assertions so the grader can score each independently.

suggestions.assertion: The executor's transcript shows evidence of a quality-gate checklist being applied before final delivery
suggestions.reason: Without access to a transcript (only an executor summary was provided), this assertion can only be inferred. If transcript access is unreliable, the assertion should be rephrased to check for an artefact in the outputs (e.g., a QA_CHECKLIST.md or a checklist section in COMPANY.md).

No assertion checks for Portman Group / CAP Code advertising compliance — a UK alcohol-specific risk that a mature brewery agent company should flag. Consider adding: 'ecommerce-operator OR brand-director mentions Portman Group, CAP Code, or alcohol advertising restrictions'. Baseline missed this; with-skill run would be evaluated fairly against it.

Ownership map consistency assertion (#20) passed despite the baseline lacking Owns subsections at all. It passed because domain sections loosely align with COMPANY.md, but the assertion is meant to verify structured Owns fields. Tighten it to require strict header-level 'Owns' subsection matching.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions discriminate well between baseline (v1-style) and v2.1 skill output — baseline passes only the minimal structural items (one file per agent, ownership non-overlap, 3-agent challenge, SEO, English). Every v2.1-specific assertion (memory, 6-section schema, Standing Instructions, COMPANY.md back-reference) fails as expected. Pass rate of 25% reflects genuine skill value.
